skill.name: writing-plans
skill.description: Create detailed implementation plans from requirements before code changes begin.
skill.summary: Turns feature specs into small, test-driven implementation tasks with file paths, commands, and handoff steps.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptographic patterns, but the cited lines are Markdown code fences, inline code, front matter, and prose. No executable script, crypto API use, network access, or data exfiltration was found in SKILL.md. A low-severity caution remains because the skill asks generated plans to include downstream Claude sub-skill directives.

risk_factor_evidence.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown syntax and command examples inside documentation. SKILL.md contains no executable Ruby, shell wrapper, or script entry point that would run these commands.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The backtick detections are inline Markdown paths, Markdown fences, and example commands for future plans. They are not executable code in this skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited location is within Markdown prose or a fenced example. There is no shell execution API or runtime script in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain front matter, ordinary planning prose, and a Goal placeholder. No weak hash, cipher, password storage, or cryptographic operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is natural language or a Markdown placeholder. No cryptographic library, algorithm name, key material, or hashing behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Generated Plans Can Carry Downstream AI Directives
low_findings.description: The plan template tells future Claude sessions to use specific sub-skills. This is an intended workflow handoff, but generated plans should be treated as instructions from an untrusted document.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The directive text is explicit and can influence later AI sessions. It does not contain override language, credential requests, network behavior, or instructions to bypass security review.

content.value_statement: Complex coding tasks fail when context, tests, and file changes are unclear. This skill turns requirements into a step-by-step implementation plan with exact tasks, commands, and handoff options.
actual_capabilities: Creates implementation plans before code changes begin.
Breaks work into small tasks designed for two to five minute steps.
Prompts for exact file paths, tests, commands, and expected results.
Encourages test-driven development and frequent commits.
Adds plan headers with goal, architecture, and tech stack sections.
Includes handoff options for subagent-driven or parallel execution.
limitations: Does not execute the plan or modify code by itself.
Requires a clear spec or requirements from the user.
Assumes work happens in a dedicated worktree created elsewhere.
References sub-skills that may not be installed in every environment.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Convert a product spec into ordered engineering tasks with tests, files, and commands.

use_cases.title: Prepare Work for Delegation
use_cases.description: Create a detailed plan that another coding agent or teammate can follow with limited context.

use_cases.title: Structure Test-Driven Changes
use_cases.description: Turn unclear requirements into failing tests, minimal implementation steps, verification, and commits.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Small Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a focused change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create an implementation plan for this requirement: [describe the change]. Include files, tests, commands, and expected results.

prompt_templates.title: Plan From a Product Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a step-by-step implementation plan from this spec: [paste spec]. Break the work into small TDD tasks and include execution handoff options.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Cross-File Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for this refactor: [describe behavior and constraints]. Identify exact files, tests to add or update, migration concerns, and commit checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Plan for Agent Execution
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced handoff to another AI coding session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a complete implementation plan for agent execution. Assume the implementer has no project context. Include architecture notes, task order, verification commands, and handoff guidance.

output_examples.input: A request to add validation to a settings form.
output_examples.output: A plan with a goal, architecture notes, affected files, failing validation tests, implementation steps, verification commands, and a commit checkpoint.

output_examples.input: A requirement to refactor authentication error handling.
output_examples.output: A task sequence that maps current files, adds regression tests, changes error handling in small steps, runs targeted tests, and records expected outcomes.

output_examples.input: A spec for delegating a feature to another coding agent.
output_examples.output: A saved plan path, task-by-task execution notes, required context, and two handoff options for continuing implementation.

best_practices: Provide the feature spec, relevant constraints, and any known file paths before asking for a plan.
Review generated commands and sub-skill references before using the plan in another AI session.
Keep each task small enough to test, verify, and commit independently.
anti_patterns: Using the skill when you only need a quick answer instead of an implementation plan.
Accepting placeholder file paths without checking them against the repository.
Skipping tests or verification steps because the plan looks complete.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a structured implementation plan with goals, architecture notes, tasks, files, tests, commands, and handoff options.

faq.question: Does it change code?
faq.answer: No. It is a planning skill and does not execute commands or edit files by itself.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before a multi-step coding task when the requirements need structure and verification steps.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports Codex, Claude, and Claude Code workflows when the environment can read the skill instructions.

faq.question: Why does it mention tests and commits?
faq.answer: The skill is designed around small test-driven steps and frequent checkpoints to reduce implementation risk.

faq.question: What should I review before executing a plan?
faq.answer: Check file paths, commands, expected results, and any downstream AI directives before using the plan for implementation.
